items.q: What is the difference between ozone depletion and climate change?
items.correct: Two distinct issues - ozone depletion is about UV protection (caused mainly by CFCs); climate change is about heat trapping (caused mainly by CO2).
items.random_wrong: Ozone depletion causes climate change directly.
items.why: Students conflate these as 'the atmosphere problem'. They involve different gases, different atmospheric layers, different mechanisms, and different (though sometimes connected) environmental consequences.

items.q: Why is a cold winter not evidence against climate change?
items.correct: Climate refers to long-term averages, not individual weather events.
items.misconception: It is evidence - if it gets cold sometimes, warming must be wrong.
items.random_wrong: Cold winters are caused by warming.
items.why: Students conflate weather and climate. Climate is the LONG-TERM pattern (decades+); weather is short-term. Global warming refers to average trends - it allows individual cold events even as overall temperatures rise.

items.correct: Water vapour released from plant leaves to the atmosphere.
items.misconception: Water flowing through soil into rivers.
items.why: Students confuse transpiration with other water movements. It's specifically water LOSS THROUGH PLANTS - significant in forested areas, contributing substantially to atmospheric water vapour over land.

items.q: Bacteria converting ammonium to nitrate in soil:
items.why: Correct answer: Nitrosomonas and Nitrobacter. “Rhizobium” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Typical energy transfer efficiency between trophic levels:
items.why: Correct answer: 10%. “1%” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Approximate share of atmospheric nitrogen as N2 (%)?
items.why: The real figure is 78 %. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
